questions.question: What is 40% of 350?
Pointing to a photo, Reena said, 'He is the son of my mother's only son.' How is the boy related to Reena?
questions.explanation: Reena's mother's only son is Reena's brother. His son is Reena's nephew.

questions.question: Find the missing number: 3, 6, 11, 18, 27, ?
questions.explanation: The differences are 3, 5, 7, 9, 11. So 27 + 11 = 38.

questions.question: Choose the antonym of 'ANCIENT'.
questions.explanation: 'Ancient' means very old; its antonym is 'modern'.

questions.question: What does NEFT stand for?
questions.explanation: NEFT stands for National Electronic Funds Transfer, a system for one-to-one funds transfer.

questions.question: Which of the following is the minimum paid-up equity capital required for setting up a Small Finance Bank (SFB) in India, as per the revised RBI guidelines?
questions.explanation: As per the Reserve Bank of India guidelines, the minimum paid-up equity capital for Small Finance Banks (SFBs) has been increased from Rs. 100 crore to Rs. 200 crore to ensure their financial robustness.

questions.question: What percentage of Adjusted Net Bank Credit (ANBC) or Credit Equivalent Amount of Off-Balance Sheet Exposure, whichever is higher, is the Priority Sector Lending (PSL) target for Domestic Scheduled Commercial Banks in India?
questions.explanation: Domestic Scheduled Commercial Banks (excluding RRBs and SFBs) are mandated by the RBI to allocate 40% of their Adjusted Net Bank Credit (ANBC) or Credit Equivalent Amount of Off-Balance Sheet Exposure, whichever is higher, to the priority sector.

questions.question: What is the revised annual premium payable by the subscriber under the Pradhan Mantri Suraksha Bima Yojana (PMSBY), effective from June 1, 2022?
questions.explanation: The annual premium for the Pradhan Mantri Suraksha Bima Yojana (PMSBY), an accident insurance scheme, was revised from Rs. 12 to Rs. 20 per annum with effect from June 1, 2022.

questions.question: Which Article of the Constitution of India mandates the presentation of the 'Annual Financial Statement' (commonly known as the Union Budget) in Parliament?
questions.explanation: According to Article 112 of the Indian Constitution, the President shall in respect of every financial year cause to be laid before both the Houses of Parliament a statement of the estimated receipts and expenditure of the Government of India, referred to as the 'Annual Financial Statement'.

questions.question: In which year did the Reserve Bank of India (RBI) commence its operations, and under which legislative Act was it established?
questions.explanation: The Reserve Bank of India was established on April 1, 1935, under the provisions of the Reserve Bank of India Act, 1934, following the recommendations of the Hilton Young Commission.

questions.question: Which of the following Indian states shares its geographical border with the maximum number of other Indian states?
questions.explanation: Uttar Pradesh shares its borders with the maximum number of states. It is bordered by 8 states (Uttarakhand, Himachal Pradesh, Haryana, Rajasthan, Madhya Pradesh, Chhattisgarh, Jharkhand, Bihar) and 1 Union Territory (Delhi).

questions.question: Which of the following vitamins is water-soluble and cannot be stored in the body for long periods?
questions.explanation: Vitamins are classified as either fat-soluble (A, D, E, K) or water-soluble (B-complex vitamins and Vitamin C). Water-soluble vitamins dissolve in water and are readily excreted in urine, meaning they must be consumed regularly.

questions.question: The headquarters of the Asian Infrastructure Investment Bank (AIIB) is located in which of the following cities?
questions.explanation: The Asian Infrastructure Investment Bank (AIIB) is a multilateral development bank headquartered in Beijing, China, which initiated its operations in January 2016.

questions.question: Under the Reserve Bank - Integrated Ombudsman Scheme, 2021, what is the maximum limit of compensation the Ombudsman can award for any loss directly suffered by the complainant?
questions.explanation: Under the Integrated Ombudsman Scheme, 2021, the Ombudsman has the power to award compensation up to Rs. 20 lakhs for any consequential financial loss directly suffered by the complainant, and up to Rs. 1 lakh for mental agony and harassment.

questions.question: Which of the following committees recommended the establishment of Regional Rural Banks (RRBs) in India to cater to rural credit needs?
questions.explanation: The Narasimham Working Group (1975) recommended the establishment of Regional Rural Banks (RRBs) to bridge the credit gap in rural areas. Consequently, the first five RRBs were set up on October 2, 1975.

questions.question: Which of the following is the minimum paid-up equity capital required for setting up a Small Finance Bank (SFB) in India, as per the latest RBI guidelines?
questions.explanation: As per the RBI's guidelines, the minimum paid-up voting equity capital for setting up a Small Finance Bank (SFB) has been increased from Rs. 100 crore to Rs. 200 crore.

questions.question: Which committee recommended the introduction of the Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) framework in India to decide policy rates?
questions.explanation: The Urjit Patel Committee (Committee to Revise and Strengthen the Monetary Policy Framework), which submitted its report in 2014, recommended the establishment of the Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) in India to bring transparency and accountability.

questions.question: Under the Pradhan Mantri Suraksha Bima Yojana (PMSBY), what is the annual premium payable by the subscriber for an accidental death and full disability cover of Rs. 2 Lakh?
questions.explanation: The annual premium for the Pradhan Mantri Suraksha Bima Yojana (PMSBY) was revised from Rs. 12 to Rs. 20 with effect from June 1, 2022. It offers an accidental death and full disability cover of Rs. 2 Lakh.

questions.question: Under which Article of the Constitution of India is the Annual Financial Statement (Union Budget) presented to the Parliament?
questions.explanation: Article 112 of the Indian Constitution deals with the Annual Financial Statement, which is popularly referred to as the Union Budget.

questions.question: Through which of the following Indian states does the Tropic of Cancer NOT pass?
questions.explanation: The Tropic of Cancer passes through 8 Indian states: Gujarat, Rajasthan, Madhya Pradesh, Chhattisgarh, Jharkhand, West Bengal, Tripura, and Mizoram. It does not pass through Odisha.

questions.question: During the Viceroyalty of whom was the Ancient Monuments Preservation Act passed in 1904?
questions.explanation: The Ancient Monuments Preservation Act was passed in 1904 during the tenure of Lord Curzon, who served as the Viceroy of India from 1899 to 1905.

questions.question: Which of the following diseases is caused by the deficiency of Vitamin D in children?
questions.explanation: Rickets is a skeletal disorder caused by a lack of vitamin D, calcium, or phosphate, primarily affecting growing children and leading to soft and weak bones.

questions.question: The head office of the International Fund for Agricultural Development (IFAD) is located in which city?
questions.explanation: The International Fund for Agricultural Development (IFAD) is a specialized agency of the United Nations dedicated to eradicating poverty and hunger in rural areas, headquartered in Rome, Italy.

questions.question: In the context of banking regulation in India, what does the acronym 'LEI' stand for, which is a 20-digit unique code used to identify parties to financial transactions globally?
questions.explanation: The Legal Entity Identifier (LEI) is a 20-character, alpha-numeric code based on the ISO 17442 standard. RBI has mandated LEI for non-individual entities undertaking high-value transactions to improve financial data systems.

questions.question: Which of the following indexes is published by the NITI Aayog to measure the performance of states and union territories in terms of water management?
questions.explanation: The Composite Water Management Index (CWMI) is launched by NITI Aayog to provide an annual snapshot of water management performance across Indian states and Union Territories.

questions.question: Under whose reign in the Delhi Sultanate was the dedicated department for agricultural development, 'Diwan-i-Amir-Kohi', established?
questions.explanation: Muhammad bin Tughlaq established the department of 'Diwan-i-Amir-Kohi' to bring uncultivated land under cultivation through direct state support and loans to farmers.

questions.question: Who among the following was the President of the Indian National Congress during the historic 1916 Lucknow Session, where the Lucknow Pact was signed?
questions.explanation: Ambica Charan Mazumdar presided over the 1916 Lucknow Session of the Indian National Congress, which marked the reunion of the moderate and extremist factions as well as the alliance with the Muslim League.

questions.question: The Barren Island, which contains the only active volcano in the Indian subcontinent, is located in which of the following water bodies?
questions.explanation: Barren Island is located in the Andaman Sea, which is a marginal sea of the northeastern Indian Ocean and part of the Bay of Bengal.

questions.question: Which of the following landlocked countries in South America is bordered exclusively by Brazil, Argentina, and Bolivia?
questions.explanation: Paraguay is one of the two landlocked nations in South America and shares its borders strictly with Bolivia to the northwest, Brazil to the northeast, and Argentina to the south and west.

questions.question: What is the scientific term used to describe the boundary zone between the Earth's crust and the upper mantle where seismic wave velocities change abruptly?
questions.explanation: The Mohorovicic Discontinuity, commonly called the Moho, is the boundary layer separating the Earth's crust from the underlying mantle.

questions.question: Which of the following laws of physics states that the total current entering a junction or node in an electrical circuit is equal to the total current leaving that node?
questions.explanation: Kirchhoff's Current Law (KCL) is based on the law of conservation of electric charge, stating that the algebraic sum of currents meeting at any junction is zero.

questions.question: What is the primary mineral constituent of 'Lapis Lazuli', a deep-blue metamorphic rock used extensively as a semi-precious stone?
questions.explanation: Lazurite is a blue feldspathoid silicate mineral and is the main component of the gemstone lapis lazuli, which gives it its signature rich blue color.

questions.question: Which of the following endocrine glands in the human body is referred to as the 'Master Gland' because it regulates the functions of other endocrine glands?
questions.explanation: The pituitary gland is called the 'master gland' because it secretes hormones that trigger other endocrine glands (like the thyroid, adrenal glands, and gonads) to produce their own hormones.

questions.question: The famous Battle of Talikota (1565 AD), which led to the collapse of the Vijayanagara Empire, was fought during the reign of which nominal Emperor of Vijayanagara?
questions.explanation: While the de facto control of the empire was in the hands of the prime minister Aliya Rama Raya (who was killed in the battle), the nominal Emperor of Vijayanagara at the time was Sadasiva Raya.

questions.question: In which year was the controversial 'Vernacular Press Act' enacted by Viceroy Lord Lytton to curtail the freedom of Indian-language newspapers?
questions.explanation: The Vernacular Press Act was passed in 1878 to suppress local-language newspapers that were critical of British policies, particularly the Second Anglo-Afghan War.

questions.question: A, B and C start a business. A invests Rs. 4000 for 12 months, B invests Rs. 6000 for 8 months, and C invests Rs. 8000 for 9 months. If the total profit at the end of the year is Rs. 21,000, find the share of C in the profit.
questions.explanation: The ratio of profit sharing is equivalent to the ratio of the product of investment and time period. Ratio of A : B : C = (4000 * 12) : (6000 * 8) : (8000 * 9) = 48000 : 48000 : 72000 = 2 : 2 : 3. Total profit shares = 2 + 2 + 3 = 7 parts. Total profit is Rs. 21,000. Therefore, 1 part = 21,000 / 7 = Rs. 3,000. C's share (3 parts) = 3 * 3,000 = Rs. 9,000.

questions.question: A and B together can complete a work in 12 days. B and C together can complete the same work in 16 days. A worked at it for 5 days, B for 7 days, and C finished the remaining work in 13 days. In how many days can C alone complete the work?
questions.explanation: Let the total work be the LCM of 12 and 16, which is 48 units. Efficiency of (A + B) = 48 / 12 = 4 units/day. Efficiency of (B + C) = 48 / 16 = 3 units/day. A worked for 5 days, B for 7 days, and C for 13 days. We can regroup this work as: 5 days of (A + B) + 2 days of (B + C) + 11 days of C. Work done by (A + B) in 5 days = 5 * 4 = 20 units. Work done by (B + C) in 2 days = 2 * 3 = 6 units. Total work done so far = 26 units. Remaining work = 48 - 26 = 22 units. This remaining 22 units is completed by C in 11 days. So, efficiency of C = 22 / 11 = 2 units/day. Time taken by C alone to complete the work = 48 / 2 = 24 days.

questions.question:
Identify the segment in the sentence which contains a grammatical error.

'Neither the manager nor his subordinates was present at the meeting to address the clients' grievances.'
questions.explanation: When two subjects are joined by 'neither... nor', the verb must agree with the closer subject. In this sentence, the closer subject is 'subordinates' (plural), so the verb should be 'were' instead of 'was'.

questions.question:
In the following sentence, a blank is given. Choose the most appropriate word from the options to fill the blank and make the sentence grammatically correct and meaningful.

'The central bank’s decision to raise interest rates was met with ________ resistance from local businesses, who feared a sharp decline in consumer spending.'
questions.explanation: 'Stiff' is the most appropriate word here as 'stiff resistance' is a standard collocation meaning strong or firm opposition. The context of fearing a 'sharp decline' indicates that the resistance from businesses would be intense and strong, making 'stiff' the best fit over placid (calm), marginal (minor), or ephemeral (short-lived).

questions.question: Under the Sovereign Gold Bond (SGB) Scheme, what is the maximum limit of investment/subscription permitted for a Hindu Undivided Family (HUF) per fiscal year (April-March)?
questions.explanation: According to the Reserve Bank of India (RBI) guidelines, the maximum limit of subscription under the Sovereign Gold Bond (SGB) Scheme is 4 kg for individuals, 4 kg for Hindu Undivided Families (HUF), and 20 kg for trusts and similar entities per fiscal year.

questions.question: In April 2022, the Reserve Bank of India (RBI) introduced the Standing Deposit Facility (SDF). Which of the following is a key characteristic of the SDF that distinguishes it from the traditional Reverse Repo facility?
options: It requires the RBI to pledge Government Securities as collateral to the depositing banks.
It operates as a liquidity absorption tool that does not require the RBI to provide collateral.
It is a lending tool rather than a deposit tool, used to inject liquidity into the banking system.
It is strictly a long-term deposit scheme with a minimum maturity period of one year.
questions.explanation: The Standing Deposit Facility (SDF) is a collateral-free liquidity absorption mechanism. Unlike the traditional Reverse Repo facility, where the RBI has to provide government securities as collateral to banks, the SDF does not require any collateral, thereby strengthening the operating framework of monetary policy.
